title: Savinja Statistical Region

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Statistical region in Slovenia

Statistical region

| Savinja Statistical Region  *savinjska statistična regija* | |
| --- | --- |
| [Statistical region](/wiki/Statistical_regions_of_Slovenia "Statistical regions of Slovenia") | |

| Area | |
| • Total | 2,384 km2 (920 sq mi) |
| Population (2020) | |

| • Density | 110.5/km2 (286.1/sq mi) |
| Statistics | |

| • Employed | 89,352 |

| • College/university students | 10,441 |
| • Regional GDP (2019): | [EUR](/wiki/Euro "Euro") 5,393 bn (EUR 20,954 per capita) |
| [HDI](/wiki/Human_Development_Index "Human Development Index") (2022) | 0.903[[1]](#cite_note-GlobalDataLab-1) very high · [6th](/wiki/List_of_Slovenian_regions_by_Human_Development_Index "List of Slovenian regions by Human Development Index") |

The **Savinja Statistical Region**[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) ([Slovene](/wiki/Slovene_language "Slovene language"): *savinjska statistična regija*) is a [statistical region](/wiki/Statistical_regions_of_Slovenia "Statistical regions of Slovenia") in [Slovenia](/wiki/Slovenia "Slovenia"). The largest town in the region is [Celje](/wiki/Celje "Celje"). It is named after the [Savinja](/wiki/Savinja "Savinja") River. The region is very diverse in natural geography; it mainly comprises the wooded mountainous terrain attractive to tourists (the Upper Savinja Valley and part of the Kamnik–Savinja Alps), the fertile Lower Savinja Valley with good conditions for growing hops, the Kozje Hills, and the Velenje Basin with lignite deposits, used for electricity production. In 2013 the region invested more than EUR 127 million in environmental protection (the most of all regions). In 2013, the region accounted for 14% of enterprises created and 8% of enterprises shut down. The region has good natural conditions for agriculture. In 2013 this region had more than 11,000 farms, which is 15% of all farms in Slovenia, ranking the region right behind the [Drava Statistical Region](/wiki/Drava_Statistical_Region "Drava Statistical Region"). In agricultural area utilised and livestock, the region was also in second place. The region is a well-known and popular tourist destination. In 2012, tourist arrivals and overnight stays in the region represented 11.1% of all tourist arrivals in Slovenia and 15.0% of all overnight stays. On average, tourists spent four nights there.

## Cities and towns

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Savinja_Statistical_Region&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Cities and towns")]

The Savinja Statistical Region includes 9 [cities](/wiki/City "City") and [towns](/wiki/Town "Town"), the largest of which is [Celje](/wiki/Celje "Celje").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Administrative divisions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Savinja_Statistical_Region&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Administrative divisions")]

The Savinja Statistical Region comprises the following 31 [municipalities](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Slovenia "Municipalities of Slovenia"):

* [Braslovče](/wiki/Municipality_of_Braslov%C4%8De "Municipality of Braslovče")

The municipalities of [Bistrica ob Sotli](/wiki/Municipality_of_Bistrica_ob_Sotli "Municipality of Bistrica ob Sotli") and the [Radeče](/wiki/Municipality_of_Rade%C4%8De "Municipality of Radeče") were part of the region until January 2015; they became part of the [Lower Sava Statistical Region](/wiki/Lower_Sava_Statistical_Region "Lower Sava Statistical Region") in 2015.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Savinja_Statistical_Region&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Demographics")]

The population in 2020 was 263,322. It has a total area of 2,301 km².

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Savinja_Statistical_Region&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Economy")]

Employment structure: 51.8% services, 45.6% industry, 2.6% agriculture.

### Tourism

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Savinja_Statistical_Region&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Tourism")]

It attracts 10.4% of the total number of tourists in Slovenia, most being from Slovenia (52.8%).

## Transportation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Savinja_Statistical_Region&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Transportation")]

* Length of motorways: 64.8 km
* Length of other roads: 5754.3 km
